titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Mills Family Papers
titlestmt.author: Riley Harrison

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 18:15

did.abstract: This collection contains materials relating to the Mills family during their lives as Presbyterian missionaries in China. The Mills family resided in China during the Nanjing Incident of 1927, the Nanjing Massacre of 1937, and World War II. The collection provides insight into China in the twentieth century containing narratives of conflict, experiences of imprisonment during the Korean War, and further descriptions of daily life as missionaries in China.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Mills Family Papers (RG 339), Special Collections, Yale Divinity Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Wilson Plumer Mills (1883-1959) was born in Winnsboro, South Carolina, and received college degrees from Davidson College, the University of South Carolina, the University of Oxford, Columbia Theological Seminary, and Union Theological Seminary. He married Cornelia Seyle Mills (1889 - 1997) in 1912 and had two children, Harriet Mills (1920 - 2016) and Mary Agnes "Angie" Mills (b. 1924).

Wilson Plumer Mills served as the YMCA secretary at the University of South Carolina before traveling to China where he served under the International Committee of YMCA from 1912 to 1932. In 1932, he resigned from the YMCA, was ordained into the Presbyterian Church, and served under the Presbyterian Foreign Mission Board in Nanjing. During this time, Cornelia Seyle Mills taught English and math in Nanking American schools, while working with Chinese women on improving healthy living conditions. While in Guling (Kuling) and Shanghai during the Sino-Japanese War, she continued teaching.

In 1937, when the Japanese army occupied Nanjing, W. Plumer Mills became vice-chairman of the International Committee for the Nanking Safety Zone, later becoming chairman. He remained in Nanjing during the Nanjing Massacre (Rape of Nanking) of 1937. During World War II, W. Plumer Mills was interned in 1941 and repatriated in 1943. He spent the next six years in Chongqing (Chungking) and Shanghai, returning to the United States in 1949. During this time, Harriet Mills resided in China as a Fulbright scholar at Peking University. After having her exit visa application rejected multiple times during the Korean War, she was imprisoned from 1951 to 1955, before returning to the United States upon release.

Harriet Mills and Angie Mills both graduated from Wellesley College. Harriet Mills went on to teach Chinese language and literature at several universities, traveling back to China to give talks and visit friends. Angie Mills researched the history of Shanghai and the Shanghai American School, returned to Shanghai to speak, and wrote about her own experiences of life in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Mills family resided as Presbyterian missionaries in Nanjing (Nanking), China from 1912 - 1943, witnessing the Nanjing Incident (1927) and the Nanjing Massacre (1937), intermittently seeking refuge in Shanghai and traveling back to the United States. W. Plumer Mills remained in China until 1949, and his daughter Harriet Mills was imprisoned in China from 1950 - 1955. The collection includes correspondence, collected materials, writings, biographical information, and photographs, providing insight into the Mills family's lives in China and the United States.

Series I, Correspondence, contains mainly family correspondence from 1913 - 2019. The series features letters sent and received during the Nanjing Incident, the Nanjing Massacre, and World War II. It also includes letters to Harriet Mills during her time imprisoned and telegrams bringing news of her release. The end of the series contains notes written by Angie Mills regarding correspondence.

Series II, Reports and Collected Materials, contains six subseries. The first subseries includes reports written by W. Plumer Mills in 1914 and 1927 regarding the Nanjing Incident. It also includes reports written by various organizations during the Nanjing Massacre in 1937. The second subseries contains personal accounts of the Nanjing Incident in 1927, including by W. Plumer Mills. The third subseries contains newspaper clippings regarding news of the Nanjing Massacre, as well as release of prisoners in 1955. Subseries four and five contain collected materials such as booklets and invitations. The sixth subseries contains information and photographs regarding Hillcrest American School in Nanking.

Series III, Writings, is organized into five subseries. The first three subseries contain writings by Harriet Mills and Angie Mills, including a last will and testament written by Harriet Mills in 1950 before her imprisonment and regarding the Mills family's life in China. The fourth subseries contains notes and drafts of a children's book, "Raffy," written and edited by the Mills family. The last subseries, titled "Miscellaneous Writings," includes notes and a notebook belonging to Cornelia Seyle Mills.

Series IV, Biographical Information, includes miscellaneous family information as well as a chronology of the lives of Wilson Plumer Mills and Cornelia Seyle Mills. In addition, the series includes identification cards of Mills family members that were used to travel between Nanjing and Shanghai.
Series V, Photographs, contains photographs of the Mills family from 1907 - 2020. The series includes a photo album with photographs dating from 1907 - 1936 and photographs of family life in Nanjing.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Place names were modernized in the description, with the name originally used in the collection material or in an older version of the finding aid in parenthesis: e.g. "Beijing (Peking)" or "Benin (Dahomey)".

subject.part: Nanking Massacre, Nanjing, Jiangsu Sheng, China, 1937

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including regarding the Nanjing Incident.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from Wilson Plumer Mills (WPM) China-Japan conflicts and a list of foreign residents in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing a letter from WPM to Mary Mills regarding Nanjing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including postcards to Harriet Mills (HM) and Mary Agnes "Angie" Mills (AM) and a letter from Kyle Adams to Mary Mills.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including regarding missionary life in China and from WPM and Cornelia "Nina" Seyle Mills (CSM) to Mary Seyle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to HM regarding being appointed editor-in-chief of Shanghai American School.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from Margaret Thomson to Friends of Plainfield.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing letters to Mary Mills from WPM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to HM from WPM regarding the war in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing letters to HM regarding Japanese extending operations to Shanghai.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing letters to HM from WPM, CSM regarding bombing in Nanjing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence regarding WPM updates of Nanjing air raids.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including letters regarding the Nanjing safety zone, CSM updates on life in Shanghai.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from the chairman of the Nanjing International Relief Committee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to CSM from WPM with "Intro to Notes on Nanking Economies," regarding safety zone committee, and to Mary Mills from Macy Smith on John Rabe's talk titled "Tiffin-Foreign YMCA Shanghai."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing letters about WPM's relief work in Nanking.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to CSM to WPM regarding filing a protest with the Japanese consulate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence regarding WPM's visit to the embassy, Hankow safety zone.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to HM from WPM regarding the Russian-Japanese situation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence regarding the possibility of war in Europe and the effects on China, and a letter on Japanese troops continuing to Hankow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence regarding fall of Hankow and Canton, CSM returning to Nanjing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to Mary Mills from WPM regarding goals of bringing Protestants and Catholics together and from HM regarding her application at Union Theological Seminary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence regarding CSM's daily life in Nanjing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including letters about returning to the U.S., and regarding the Chinese New Year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to WPM, CSM from HM regarding release of detained International Committee men.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing WPM's summary of assets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to daughters from CSM aboard the S.S. President Cleveland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence regarding the closing of Shanghai American School and updates on leaving Nanjing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to Dr. Ruland from WPM, regarding Presbyterian Board sending people back to the U.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including family correspondence regarding CSM arriving in New York and letter to AM from Soozi of Shanghai school updates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence regarding evacuation news from Methodist Board.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to HM from John Day Company Inc. Publishers, postcard to AM from HM, and a letter with document titled "Market Prices."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to CSM from WPM regarding the International Relief Committee, to the Council Office from Mission and Station Secretaries regarding a request for missionaries to remain in China, to CSM from WPM regarding the Executive Committee of Missions request for CSM to return to China for work in fall of 1941 .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence regarding global events including tension between the U.S. and Japan, the appointment of American Military Mission in China, and the possibility of CSM returning to China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing correspondence between AM and HM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing postcards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including the China Bulletin from Board of Foreign Missions of Presbyterian Church in the U.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including the China Bulletin from Board of Foreign Missions of Presbyterian Church in the U.S., to HM from Rowland & Edith, and to AM from WPM about a trip to New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including letters of CSM's travels in India.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including letters to WPM regarding return to China, to CSM from WPM regarding National Christian Council of Chungking, and regarding the Chinese-American Institute of Cultural Relations' annual meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family correspondence regarding the war in Europe and the Pacific, notices of group meetings in China, and the meaning of the end of WWII in Europe for China's future.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including regarding Japanese surrender in China, to Lack family from Mrs. Lack regarding their release from Weihsien Internment Camp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to HM from WPM with a U.S. Information Services clipping and regarding a potential Chinese civil war.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including newspaper feature "Shanghai's Future" and a letter regarding life in Shanghai after the war and regarding a truce between central government of China and the communists.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to CSM, AM from HM aboard S.S. General Meigs to China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from HM regarding her arrival in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from HM to "people" regarding travels and starting school.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to family from HM regarding the College of Chinese Studies in "Peiping," including a photograph of HM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to Edward Mills from WPM regarding Foreign Missions Conference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to family from HM regarding student massacre in Beijing and warrant for students' arrests.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from HM regarding worsening military situation in China, staying in Beijing, and regarding WPM's travels to Shanghai.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to CSM from HM on Beijing surrendering to the communists and U.S. marines in Shanghai.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing updates on the war in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including regarding WPM traveling from Shanghai to San Francisco and regarding the communist party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing letters to the family from HM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing letters to Edward and May from WPM and AM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from HM with photographs of life in China and information on the Korea Campaign.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing letters to CSM from HM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including letters to family from HM regarding inability to leave China and a letter to CSM from James Hightower

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including a telegram on the witholding of HM's permit to leave China and to CSM regarding "code" of dates written on HM's writing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to CSM from Mrs. E.J. Shaw regarding news of HM's arrest and to WPM from the American Presbyterian Mission regarding political prisons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing letters to HM regarding hopes of release.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to WPM from Legation of Switzerland regarding no news of HM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from HM in the Peking Prison and from the Department of State regarding HM's well-being.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing a letter via National Red Cross Society of China from HM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including letters hoping for HM's release.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including telegrams regarding HM's release and letters from HM aboard S.S. American Mail.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to HM from various people regarding news of her return to the U.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing letters to CSM from HM regarding travels back to China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including regarding the Mills' Guling home.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to Nanjing friends from HM regarding being back in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from HM regarding book research in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including copied letters to Anna regarding Hillcrest School in Nanjing in the 1930s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including email correspondence between AM and Dylan Nelson regarding making a film of WPM's life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing a letter to Tammy from AM including write-up and chronology of WPM's life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing notes on correspondence "Regarding the Rape of Nanking", "Names & Places Referred to in Mills' Letters"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing notes on correspondence from July 25, 1937 to April 29, 1941.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing notes on correspondence from 1913 - 1981.

did.unittitle: "Annual Report for the Year Ending September 14, 1914" by Wilson Plumer Mills
"The Anti-Christian Movement from Another Angle" by The China Press
"A Report of Recent Conversations with Chinese Friends" by Wilson Plumer Mills

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Public Statement by A.J. Bowen, LL.D., Methodist Episcopal Mission, and President of University of Nanking," including a list of Nanjing residents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "A Brief Statement of the Far Eastern Situation" and a report from China Mission Secretary.

scopecontent.p: Including reports by the Emergency Committee of University of Nanking and a journal update from Nanking Safety Zone.

scopecontent.p: Including Japanese Newspaper propoganda and economic reports on Nanjing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing personal accounts by various people who experienced Nanjing Incident and notes on the accounts.

"Conversation with Consul Tanaka with M.S. Bates"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including articles on the Rape of Nanjing, the Shanghai American School newspaper, and the CIFRC Bulletin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing articles regarding release of prisoners in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including articles "Nanking 50 Years After."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including on Japanese war crimes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Harriet Mills' address given at the event.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including a booklet titled "Some Problems Confronting the Christian Movement in China."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including event invitations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including original photographs, pamphlets, and emails.

did.unittitle: "Seeing Nanking: Our Own Geography" by Hillcrest School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing a booklet on Nanjing addressed to Harriet Mills.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Further writings are in Series II, Reports and Collected Materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing the last will written by HM during the war.

did.unittitle: "Thought Reform: Ideological Remold in China" by Harriet Mills

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing an article in The Atlantic by HM.

did.unittitle: "Reflections on a Missionary Childhood" by Harriet Mills
Timeline and background notes by Harriet Mills

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including a "Time Table" of HM's arrest in China and personal/family background on time in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing AM's research and drafts for a synopsis on Shanghai edited by HM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including AM's notes on the history of Shanghai and on the Shanghai American School, and edits by HM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including outlines and various chapter drafts titled: "1912 First Impressions", "RFD 1931-1932: Tunk/Farm", "RFD 1929-1931: BT Jie Pets/Silk", "1932-1937 Morning Readings", "Shopper."

did.unittitle: Notes regarding TV documentary of Rape of Nanjing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing materials and notes regarding CYKAO and interview questions regarding TV documentary on the Nanjing Massacre.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing a speech given by AM in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing notes for the children's book "Raffy."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Events occuring March 24, 1927

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing notes on standard oil and other stocks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including writing by CSM and notes on Chinese holidays.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing various pages from unknown writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing biographical information regarding lives of WPM and CSM.

did.unittitle: Chronology of Wilson and Cornelia Mills

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing a detailed chronology compiled by AM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing family ID cards necessary for travel from Nanjing to Shanghai.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Wilson Plumer Mills and Harriet Mills

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including photographs of WPM at Oxford and as sargent and photographs of HM as a child.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including a photograph of CSM.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including photographs of WPM and HM, Bethesda Presbyterian Church, and Nanjing.
